163	export async function copy_babel_runtime(task, opts) {
164	  const runtimeDir = dirname(require.resolve('@babel/runtime/package.json'))
165	  const outputDir = join(__dirname, 'src/compiled/@babel/runtime')
166	  const runtimeFiles = glob.sync('**/*', {
167	    cwd: runtimeDir,
168	    ignore: ['node_modules/**/*'],
169	  })
170	
171	  for (const file of runtimeFiles) {
172	    const inputPath = join(runtimeDir, file)
173	    const outputPath = join(outputDir, file)
174	
175	    if (!(await fs.stat(inputPath)).isFile()) {
176	      continue
177	    }
178	    let contents = await fs.readFile(inputPath, 'utf8')
179	
180	    if (inputPath.endsWith('.js')) {
181	      contents = contents
182	        .replace(
183	          'regenerator-runtime',
184	          'next/dist/compiled/regenerator-runtime'
185	        )
186	        .replace('@babel/runtime', 'next/dist/compiled/@babel/runtime')
187	    }
188	
189	    if (inputPath.endsWith('package.json')) {
190	      contents = JSON.stringify({
191	        ...JSON.parse(contents),
192	        dependencies: {},
193	      })
194	    }
195	
196	    await fs.mkdir(dirname(outputPath), { recursive: true })
197	    await fs.writeFile(outputPath, contents)
198	  }
199	}

## Next.js Entry Point Architecture Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the Next.js codebase, here's the complete guide for understanding how to add a new `next/geo` entry point:

### 1. **Public Entry Point Format** (`packages/next/package.json`)

The `package.json` uses a `files` array (not modern `exports` field) to declare public entry points. Each entry point requires both `.js` and `.d.ts` files at the root level:

**Examples from the files array:**
```
"files": [
  "dist",                          // compiled output directory
  "headers.js",                    // CommonJS wrapper
  "headers.d.ts",                  // TypeScript definitions
  "navigation.js",
  "navigation.d.ts",
  "server.js",
  "server.d.ts",
  "cache.js",
  "cache.d.ts",
  // ... etc
]
```

No explicit `exports` field is used. Instead, the `.js` and `.d.ts` files at the root level are the published entry points.

### 2. **Source File Locations**

Source files live in `packages/next/src/server/request/`:

| Entry Point | Source Implementation | TypeScript Def File |

1	import {
2	  HeadersAdapter,
3	  type ReadonlyHeaders,
4	} from '../web/spec-extension/adapters/headers'
5	import {
6	  workAsyncStorage,
7	  type WorkStore,
8	} from '../app-render/work-async-storage.external'
9	import {
10	  throwForMissingRequestStore,
11	  workUnitAsyncStorage,
12	  type PrerenderStoreModern,
13	  type RequestStore,
14	  isInEarlyRenderStage,
15	} from '../app-render/work-unit-async-storage.external'
16	import {
17	  postponeWithTracking,
18	  throwToInterruptStaticGeneration,
19	  trackDynamicDataInDynamicRender,
20	} from '../app-render/dynamic-rendering'
21	import { StaticGenBailoutError } from '../../client/components/static-generation-bailout'
22	import {
23	  delayUntilRuntimeStage,
24	  makeDevtoolsIOAwarePromise,
25	  makeHangingPromise,
26	} from '../dynamic-rendering-utils'
27	import { createDedupedByCallsiteServerErrorLoggerDev } from '../create-deduped-by-callsite-server-error-logger'
28	import { isRequestAPICallableInsideAfter } from './utils'
29	import { InvariantError } from '../../shared/lib/invariant-error'
30	import { RenderStage } from '../app-render/staged-rendering'
31	
32	/**
33	 * This function allows you to read the HTTP incoming request headers in
34	 * [Server Components](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/rendering/server-components),
35	 * [Server Actions](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/data-fetching/server-actions-and-mutations),
36	 * [Route Handlers](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/routing/route-handlers) and
37	 * [Middleware](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/routing/middleware).
38	 *
39	 * Read more: [Next.js Docs: `headers`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/headers)
40	 */
41	export function headers(): Promise<ReadonlyHeaders> {
42	  const callingExpression = 'headers'
43	  const workStore = workAsyncStorage.getStore()
44	  const workUnitStore = workUnitAsyncStorage.getStore()
45	
46	  if (workStore) {
47	    if (
48	      workUnitStore &&
49	      workUnitStore.phase === 'after' &&
50	      !isRequestAPICallableInsideAfter()
51	    ) {
52	      throw new Error(
53	        `Route ${workStore.route} used \`headers()\` inside \`after()\`. This is not supported. If you need this data inside an \`after()\` callback, use \`headers()\` outside of the callback. See more info here: https://nextjs.org/docs/canary/app/api-reference/functions/after`
54	      )
55	    }
56	
57	    if (workStore.forceStatic) {
58	      // When using forceStatic we override all other logic and always just return an empty
59	      // headers object without tracking
60	      const underlyingHeaders = HeadersAdapter.seal(new Headers({}))
61	      return makeUntrackedHeaders(underlyingHeaders)
62	    }
63	
64	    if (workUnitStore) {
65	      switch (workUnitStore.type) {
66	        case 'cache': {
67	          const error = new Error(
68	            `Route ${workStore.route} used \`headers()\` inside "use cache". Accessing Dynamic data sources inside a cache scope is not supported. If you need this data inside a cached function use \`headers()\` outside of the cached function and pass the required dynamic data in as an argument. See more info here: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/next-request-in-use-cache`
69	          )
70	          Error.captureStackTrace(error, headers)
71	          workStore.invalidDynamicUsageError ??= error
72	          throw error
73	        }
74	        case 'unstable-cache':
75	          throw new Error(
76	            `Route ${workStore.route} used \`headers()\` inside a function cached with \`unstable_cache()\`. Accessing Dynamic data sources inside a cache scope is not supported. If you need this data inside a cached function use \`headers()\` outside of the cached function and pass the required dynamic data in as an argument. See more info here: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/unstable_cache`
77	          )
78	        case 'generate-static-params':
79	          throw new Error(
80	            `Route ${workStore.route} used \`headers()\` inside \`generateStaticParams\`. This is not supported because \`generateStaticParams\` runs at build time without an HTTP request. Read more: https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/next-dynamic-api-wrong-context`
81	          )
82	        case 'prerender':
83	        case 'prerender-client':
84	        case 'validation-client':
85	        case 'private-cache':
86	        case 'prerender-runtime':
87	        case 'prerender-ppr':
88	        case 'prerender-legacy':
89	        case 'request':
90	          break
91	        default:
92	          workUnitStore satisfies never
93	      }
94	    }
95	
96	    if (workStore.dynamicShouldError) {
97	      throw new StaticGenBailoutError(
98	        `Route ${workStore.route} with \`dynamic = "error"\` couldn't be rendered statically because it used \`headers()\`. See more info here: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/rendering/static-and-dynamic#dynamic-rendering`
99	      )
100	    }
101	
102	    if (workUnitStore) {
103	      switch (workUnitStore.type) {
104	        case 'prerender':
105	          return makeHangingHeaders(workStore, workUnitStore)
106	        case 'prerender-client':
107	        case 'validation-client':
108	          const exportName = '`headers`'
109	          throw new InvariantError(
110	            `${exportName} must not be used within a client component. Next.js should be preventing ${exportName} from being included in client components statically, but did not in this case.`
111	          )
112	        case 'prerender-ppr':
113	          // PPR Prerender (no cacheComponents)
114	          // We are prerendering with PPR. We need track dynamic access here eagerly
115	          // to keep continuity with how headers has worked in PPR without cacheComponents.
116	          // TODO consider switching the semantic to throw on property access instead
117	          return postponeWithTracking(
118	            workStore.route,
119	            callingExpression,
120	            workUnitStore.dynamicTracking
121	          )
122	        case 'prerender-legacy':
123	          // Legacy Prerender
124	          // We are in a legacy static generation mode while prerendering
125	          // We track dynamic access here so we don't need to wrap the headers in
126	          // individual property access tracking.
127	          return throwToInterruptStaticGeneration(
128	            callingExpression,
129	            workStore,
130	            workUnitStore
131	          )
132	        case 'prerender-runtime':
133	          return delayUntilRuntimeStage(
134	            workUnitStore,
135	            makeUntrackedHeaders(workUnitStore.headers)
136	          )
137	        case 'private-cache':
138	          // Private caches are delayed until the runtime stage in use-cache-wrapper,
139	          // so we don't need an additional delay here.
140	          return makeUntrackedHeaders(workUnitStore.headers)
141	        case 'request':
142	          trackDynamicDataInDynamicRender(workUnitStore)
143	
144	          if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development') {
145	            // Semantically we only need the dev tracking when running in `next dev`
146	            // but since you would never use next dev with production NODE_ENV we use this
147	            // as a proxy so we can statically exclude this code from production builds.
148	            return makeUntrackedHeadersWithDevWarnings(
149	              workUnitStore.headers,
150	              workStore?.route,
151	              workUnitStore
152	            )
153	          } else if (workUnitStore.asyncApiPromises) {
154	            return isInEarlyRenderStage(workUnitStore)
155	              ? workUnitStore.asyncApiPromises.earlyHeaders
156	              : workUnitStore.asyncApiPromises.headers
157	          } else {
158	            return makeUntrackedHeaders(workUnitStore.headers)
159	          }
160	          break
161	        default:
162	          workUnitStore satisfies never

import { isPrivateIp } from './is-private-ip'

describe('isPrivateIp', () => {
  it('should return true for private ip addresses', () => {
    expect(isPrivateIp('127.0.0.0')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('127.0.0.1')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('127.0.0.01')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('127.0.0.001')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('0.0.0.0')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('10.0.0.0')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('10.244.0.0')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('192.168.0.0')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('192.168.0.1')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('192.168.0.01')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('172.16.0.0')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('172.16.0.1')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('172.16.0.01')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('192.168.0.1')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('192.168.0.01')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('169.254.169.254')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('::')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('::1')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('::ffff:0.0.0.0')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('::ffff:127.0.0.1')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('::ffff:7f00:1')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp(new URL('http://0x7f000001').hostname)).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('2001:2f:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('[2001:2f:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff:ffff]')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('2002::')).toBe(true)
    expect(isPrivateIp('ff00::')).toBe(true)
  })

  it('should return false for public ip addresses', () => {
    expect(isPrivateIp('76.76.21.21')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('157.240.14.35')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('8.8.8.8')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('1.1.1.1')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('::ffff:8.8.8.8')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('::ffff:1.1.1.1')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('2001:4860:4860::8888')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('2606:4700:4700::1111')).toBe(false)
  })

  it('should return false for domains', () => {
    expect(isPrivateIp('vercel.com')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('www.vercel.com')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('nextjs.org')).toBe(false)
    expect(isPrivateIp('docs.nextjs.org')).toBe(false)
  })
})
